title: symphytum davisii subsp. icaricum
intro: Symphytum davisii subsp. icaricum, also known as Icarian comfrey, is a subspecies of the comfrey plant (Symphytum davisii) that is native to Greece. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties due to the presence of allantoin, a compound that promotes tissue regeneration. The health benefits of Icarian comfrey include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce joint pain and inflammation, and aid in the healing of wounds and ulcers. Icarian comfrey is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and can also be added to topical creams and ointments for its healing properties.
